2606.02802.title: ChatHealthAI: Aligning Electronic Health Record Representations with Large Language Models for Grounded Clinical Reasoning
2606.02802.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) exhibit strong natural-language reasoning abilities for clinical decision support, but struggle to effectively model structured longitudinal electronic health records (EHRs). In contrast, EHR foundation models can learn predictive patient representations, yet lack interpretable language-based reasoning. To bridge this gap, we propose ChatHealthAI, a multimodal reasoning framework that aligns structured EHR representations from a pretrained EHR foundation model with the semantic space of a frozen LLM through a task-aware resampler. By integrating longitudinal patient representations with refined clinical event descriptions, ChatHealthAI enables clinically grounded natural-language reasoning while maintaining accurate patient prediction. We evaluated ChatHealthAI on three clinical predictive tasks from the EHRSHOT benchmark. Results show that ChatHealthAI improves reasoning quality and interpretability while preserving competitive predictive performance. These findings highlight the potential of integrating EHR foundation models with pretrained LLMs for interpretable clinical prediction.
2606.02802.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在臨床決策支持方面展現出強大的自然語言推理能力，但在有效建模結構化的縱向電子健康紀錄（EHRs）方面則面臨挑戰。相對而言，EHR 基礎模型能夠學習預測性患者表示，但缺乏可解釋的基於語言的推理。為了彌補這一差距，我們提出了 ChatHealthAI，一個多模態推理框架，通過任務感知的重採樣器將預訓練的 EHR 基礎模型中的結構化 EHR 表示與凍結的 LLM 的語義空間對齊。通過將縱向患者表示與精煉的臨床事件描述相結合，ChatHealthAI 能夠實現臨床基礎的自然語言推理，同時保持準確的患者預測。我們在 EHRSHOT 基準的三個臨床預測任務上評估了 ChatHealthAI。結果顯示，ChatHealthAI 提高了推理質量和可解釋性，同時保持了競爭性的預測性能。這些發現突顯了將 EHR 基礎模型與預訓練 LLM 結合以實現可解釋臨床預測的潛力。
2606.02802.author: Bo-Hong Wang et.al.
2606.02802.authors: Bo-Hong Wang, Baicheng Peng, Ruilin Wang, Jun Bai, Ziyang Song, Yue Li
